data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

Setup facts grounding the walk: 8937 is the keystone. Its file_impact is the schema module (`_snapshot_schema.py`, new), the joint round-trip contract test (`test_snapshot_contract.py`, new), a producer-validation edit to `fetcher.py`, and the documented anti-change-detector guardrail/ADR (`docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md`). It declares depends_on fe57 (consumes story A's fixtures/FakeAcliClient). Its round-trip drives FakeAcliClient → fetcher.fetch_snapshot → inbound_differ + outbound_differ → applier "through the PRODUCTION path."

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's schema/contract-test files are net-new; its `fetcher.py` producer-validation edit is disjoint from every sibling's edits — fe57 (`_fakes.py`/fixtures/capture script/test_jira_fixtures.py/docs), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py + reads acli_graph.py), 33d0/2359 (the outbound applier cluster: apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (probe script + test_roundtrip.py). 8937 CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient (read) over the declared depends_on fe57 edge — a clean producer/consumer split, no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's snapshot-entry schema covers exactly the unmassaged shapes the rest of the roster targets: nested `comment` (bug-0ee6 seam), `issuelinks` (bug-3f04 seam), `parent`, and scalars. This is mutually reinforcing with fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) and 59ef AC2 (the fake fails the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client) — the schema is the canonical shape both producer/consumer sides target. 8937's schema/round-trip is on the INBOUND snapshot surface; the one real cross-child representation conflict in this epic (outbound labels_applied list-vs-int between 33d0↔2359, resolved as-shipped via integer counters) is outbound-applier-internal and untouched by 8937 (its schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937 correctly declares depends_on fe57 (it needs the real-payload fixtures/fake to drive the round-trip). Its round-trip drives "...→ applier through the production path," and it does NOT declare depends_on 33d0 — but this is the same abstract-production-path binding ruled benign across the roster: the inbound comment/issuelinks/parent reads and outbound dispatch its round-trip exercises already exist in production (the epic closes a test/probe-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs), and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction. No unmet "fix-must-land-first" input edge. (Per the epic sequencing 8937 lands its thin slice on the comment/issuelinks seam first, then broadens — consistent with fe57 landing first.)
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 8937 contracts the ASSEMBLED snapshot-entry key/shape (fetcher→differ reads); 59ef contracts the enrichment-METHOD return shape (client surface); 822a asserts inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP; 33d0 asserts outbound multi-sub-op consolidation correctness. Four distinct, complementary angles on distinct files. 8937 explicitly OWNS the epic's documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact (parent AC names "owned by story B" verbatim); no sibling duplicates that deliverable — each other story only APPLIES the posture to its own tests and REFERENCES 8937's ADR. No duplicated deliverable.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (story A's fixtures/fake must exist before the contract test that consumes them) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. 8937 is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster (different files/surface entirely).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 8937 is the keystone OTHER siblings consume: its `_snapshot_schema.py` is the SSOT the fetcher/differ-validation tests key on, and its guardrail ADR is referenced by the new tests across the roster (the parent AC's "referenced by new tests" clause). Its assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), explicitly NO whole-blob golden equality and NO mock-call-count, consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added." It imposes the anti-change-detector posture by design — an ENABLING constraint, not a brittle one.
- Residual references — BENIGN. 8937 references only live surfaces: the four enrichment methods + fetch_snapshot merge points (via fe57's fake) and "the production path" applier. 33d0's deletion targets the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, NOT the inbound fetch/enrichment surface or the production batch path 8937's round-trip drives, so its deletion strands nothing 8937 references. No dangling reference.

NOTE on the anti-change-detector guardrail: unlike the recurring roster-wide AC6 discussion, this criterion is OWNED by THIS child (8937, story B), so there is no roster-level absence to attribute and no G4 cross-child inconsistency arising from it — 8937 produces the governing artifact (ADR-0004) and the siblings reference it, which is a consistent producer/consumer relationship, not a contradiction or gap.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
